content.text:
{"id":"ABA-62","title":"QA â€” Validate M6 Router acceptance criteria and milestone goals","description":"End-to-end acceptance testing for M6. All 6 checks must pass before M6 is marked Done.

## Pre-conditions

* All M4 (/stock:screen, /stock:timing) and M5 (/stock:model) skills are stable and locally installed
* All 4 M6 implementation issues (<issue id="9e804b1b-bc77-49f3-8c3f-e9d170566b4b">ABA-36</issue>, <issue id="56a1cede-df61-40bb-99de-a3682ee9153a">ABA-37</issue>, <issue id="467d964a-5c2e-463f-bf2b-d20c930c3042">ABA-38</issue>, <issue id="acaa5b76-30e2-4114-adb1-0731adbf4f2e">ABA-39</issue>) are Done
* `/stock:equity` skill file exists (e.g. `skills/equity/SKILL.md`)

## Checks

### 1\. Profit-stage inference (<issue id="9e804b1b-bc77-49f3-8c3f-e9d170566b4b">ABA-36</issue>)

Acceptance criterion: router infers profit stage and track from ticker/context without asking user to confirm.

- [ ] `/stock:equity NVDA` â†’ emits inference statement ("Inferred: established profitable tech, growth track") before dispatching to sub-skills
- [ ] `/stock:equity Anthropic` (pre-profit context) â†’ correct pre-profit inference, no confirmation prompt

### 2\. Screen â†’ Signal chain (<issue id="56a1cede-df61-40bb-99de-a3682ee9153a">ABA-37</issue>)

Acceptance criterion: router auto-proceeds to signal when screen passes, stops cleanly when it doesn't.

- [ ] `/stock:equity NVDA` with screen PASS â†’ signal invoked automatically in same response chain, no user re-invocation required
- [ ] `/stock:equity AAPL` with screen FAIL/SKIP â†’ stops after screen with explanation, no signal attempted

### 3\. MODEL_READY gate & Signal â†’ Model chain (<issue id="467d964a-5c2e-463f-bf2b-d20c930c3042">ABA-38</issue>)

Acceptance criterion: router respects the MODEL_READY flag from signal output.

- [ ] Signal returns `MODEL_READY: YES` â†’ Model invoked automatically
- [ ] Signal returns `MODEL_READY: CONDITIONAL` â†’ Router pauses and prompts user for confirmation before proceeding
- [ ] Signal returns `MODEL_READY: NO` â†’ Router stops with explanation, no model invoked

### 4\. Mid-chain entry detection (<issue id="acaa5b76-30e2-4114-adb1-0731adbf4f2e">ABA-39</issue>)

Acceptance criterion: router detects prior sub-skill output in context and skips completed stages.

- [ ] Session where `/stock:signal NVDA` already ran â†’ `/stock:equity NVDA` skips screen stage, states "Signal output found in context â€” proceeding to model", dispatches to model
- [ ] Fresh session â†’ `/stock:equity NVDA` runs full chain (screen â†’ signal â†’ model)

### 5\. End-to-end smoke test

- [ ] Full `/stock:equity NVDA` run in a clean session completes all stages and produces valid combined output

### 6\. Report JSON integration

- [ ] Router output includes sub-skill outputs (Screen, Signal, Model/Timing as applicable)
- [ ] JSON is valid and matches existing schema spec

## Sign-off

| Check | Pass / Fail | Notes |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 1. Profit-stage inference |  |  |
| 2. Screen â†’ Signal chain |  |  |
| 3. MODEL_READY gate |  |  |
| 4. Mid-chain entry detection |  |  |
| 5. End-to-end smoke test |  |  |
| 6. Report JSON integration |  |  |

{"id":"ABA-63","title":"Post-QA â€” skill-creator review and local installation check for /stock:equity","description":"After QA (<issue id="c2eaae4f-f597-444e-9d02-3469f77e282b">ABA-62</issue>) passes, run `/skill-creator:skill-creator` against the `/stock:equity` skill to surface any remaining defects, and verify the skill is correctly installed and callable locally.

## Done when

* `/skill-creator:skill-creator` has been explicitly invoked against the `/stock:equity` skill
* Any defects surfaced are fixed or filed as follow-on issues
* Skill is confirmed locally installed and callable (not just authored as a `.md` file)
* Manual invocation sanity check passes

## Checklist

- [ ] Run `/skill-creator:skill-creator` on the `/stock:equity` skill â€” review full output for defects
- [ ] Fix any defects found, or open a follow-on issue for each one with a clear description
- [ ] Confirm skill file is present in the expected local installation path
- [ ] Confirm `/stock:equity` is invocable without extra setup steps (not just a `.md` file sitting in a folder)
- [ ] Manual sanity check: `/stock:equity NVDA` runs without error in a clean session

## Blocked by

content.text:
{"id":"ABA-64","title":"QA: end-to-end UI validation suite (Playwright)","description":"Install Playwright and write an E2E suite that exercises the full report UI against fixture JSON files. Tests must run headless via `npm run test:e2e` and pass in CI.

## Fixture files

Committed to `ui/test/fixtures/`:

* `screen_only.json` â€” only `stages.screen` populated (PASS verdict)
* `signal_only.json` â€” only `stages.signal` populated (BUY verdict)
* `screen_signal.json` â€” both stages, mixed verdicts (WATCH screen, BUY signal)
* `full_report.json` â€” all four stages (screen + signal + model + timing), model_ready = YES
* `empty_stages.json` â€” root keys present but `stages: {}`

## Files to create / modify

| Path | Action |
| -- | -- |
| `ui/package.json` | Add `"test:e2e": "playwright test"` script; add `@playwright/test` devDependency |
| `ui/playwright.config.js` | New â€” configure Vite preview server, fixtures dir, headless |
| `ui/test/fixtures/*.json` | New â€” 5 fixture files covering all stage combinations |
| `ui/test/e2e/report-ui.spec.js` | New â€” 12 test cases matching acceptance criteria below |

## Acceptance criteria

Done when `npm run test:e2e` reports 12 passing tests (headless).

 1. **Scaffold** â€” `playwright.config.js` present; `npm run test:e2e` runs headless against `vite preview` with the fixtures directory substituted for `reports/`.
 2. **Empty state** â€” loading `empty_stages.json` (no stages) shows all tab buttons disabled except Summary; page renders without crashing.
 3. **Report picker** â€” switching the `<select>` to a different fixture loads the correct ticker and date in the header within one render cycle.
 4. **Screen tab** â€” loading `screen_only.json`: all five ratio values render (pe_ratio, ps_ratio, ev_ebitda, pfcf, ev_revenue); rationale text visible; verdict badge present.
 5. **Signal tab** â€” loading `signal_only.json`: all 12 signal fields render; verdict badge present; `model_ready` value visible.
 6. **Model tab** â€” loading `full_report.json`: bear/base/bull scenario values render; visual indicator shows current price relative to intrinsic value range.
 7. **Sensitivity table** â€” loading `full_report.json`: editing a WACC cell updates all cells in that column within 200 ms; base-case cell has distinct visual style.
 8. **Timing tab** â€” loading `full_report.json`: SUE score, PEAD window status, revision direction, and next catalyst all render.
 9. **Summary tab** â€” loading `full_report.json`: single BUY/WATCH/CAUTION badge renders; thesis paragraph non-empty; MODEL_READY flag visible.
10. **Verdict badge colours** â€” green applied to BUY/PASS; amber to WATCH/CONDITIONAL; red to CAUTION/SKIP. Verified via computed CSS colour value, not class name.
11. **PDF export** â€” clicking the export button fires `window.print`; test mocks `window.print` and asserts called exactly once.
12. **No console errors** â€” zero `console.error` calls during load and tab navigation for any fixture.

## Why Playwright

`import.meta.glob`, `window.print()`, and CSS computed values can't be reliably exercised by Vitest + jsdom. Playwright runs a real browser against `vite preview`, covering all of that with minimal configuration overhead.","projectMilestone":{"id":"5d1155c5-ea72-43a8-aa7f-52bb42d3eb41","name":"M7 â€” Report UI"},"priority":{"value":4,"name":"Low"},"url":"https://linear.app/ababushkin/issue/ABA-64/qa-end-to-end-ui-validation-suite-playwright","gitBranchName":"anton/aba-64-qa-end-to-end-ui-validation-suite-playwright","createdAt":"2026-05-12T11:36:53.850Z","updatedAt":"2026-05-12T11:37:36.232Z","archivedAt":null,"completedAt":null,"startedAt":null,"canceledAt":null,"dueDate":null,"slaStartedAt":null,"slaMediumRiskAt":null,"slaHighRiskAt":null,"slaBreachesAt":null,"slaType":"all","status":"Backlog","statusType":"backlog","labels":[],"attachments":[],"documents":[],"createdBy":"Anton Babushkin","createdById":"720d0535-dfc6-465f-8111-d1a3e6eab786","project":"Equity skill pack","projectId":"553fa5bd-f8cc-4a58-8236-2df12aa049d5","team":"Personal","teamId":"c20f56da-f121-4653-bf6c-dc1c8aac21c1"}

content.text:
{"id":"ABA-107","title":"KPI coverage page â€” at-a-glance HTML of every supported company + metric","description":"## What I want

A single HTML page I can open in a browser that shows, at a glance:

* Every ticker the model currently knows how to enrich with a company-specific KPI
* Which KPI family it falls under (user engagement / segment revenue / infrastructure bookings)
* What specific metric is being tracked (e.g. "Daily Active Uniques", "AWS segment revenue", "EUV net bookings")
* A link to the source filing the metric was last read from
* Which tickers are **deliberately excluded** and why (with the "revisit whenâ€¦" condition that would bring them back into scope)
* Last-checked status from the weekly drift CI â€” green / amber / red per ticker

The intent is one place I can send to someone (or check myself) to answer "does the model handle company X?" without grepping through JSON files.

## Why

Coverage is currently scattered across three JSON files (one per KPI family, only one of which exists today) plus the excluded-tickers block in each. To see the full picture you'd have to open each file and reason about it â€” fine for the maintainer, opaque to anyone else. A rendered page makes the scope visible and makes gaps obvious (e.g. "we cover 3 social companies and 0 cloud companies â€” that's the next gap to close").

It also turns the otherwise-invisible work (the excluded-tickers reasoning, the drift-CI signal, the source filings) into something visible and trustworthy.

## Source of truth

The page renders from the map files themselves â€” no separate copy of the data to maintain:

* `skills/_shared/engagement_kpi_map.json` (exists today, <issue id="32af2fee-a858-4aa2-8229-1620e8171f1f">ABA-66</issue>)
* `skills/_shared/segment_kpi_map.json` (created by <issue id="178bc70d-eda3-4e2a-956d-479c553aa7d6">ABA-65</issue>)
* `skills/_shared/bookings_kpi_map.json` (created by <issue id="d7f42e36-c909-4f2d-a494-aaf1afaf1685">ABA-67</issue>)
* Latest drift-CI state from the `engagement-kpi-drift` workflow run (and its siblings as they ship)

So the page is correct by construction â€” if a ticker is added to a map, it appears; if removed, it disappears. No documentation drift.

## What good looks like

* Open the page in a browser, see a table grouped by KPI family
* Each row: ticker, KPI name, last read value + period, source filing link, drift-CI status
* Below the main table, a "deliberately excluded" section with ticker + reason + revisit condition
* Footer shows when the page was generated and links to the milestone (M5.5) for context
* Page is static HTML â€” no server, no build step the user has to remember to run. Either committed to the repo regenerated by a GH Action, or rendered by the existing Vite + React report UI as a new tab.

## Out of scope

* Editing the maps from the page â€” read-only
* Historical view of "what KPIs were supported on date X" â€” current snapshot only
* Per-ticker drill-down with the full audit trail â€” the report JSON already does that

## Dependencies

* <issue id="32af2fee-a858-4aa2-8229-1620e8171f1f">ABA-66</issue> shipped (done) â€” engagement map exists
* For the page to show all three families: <issue id="178bc70d-eda3-4e2a-956d-479c553aa7d6">ABA-65</issue> and <issue id="d7f42e36-c909-4f2d-a494-aaf1afaf1685">ABA-67</issue> each need to land their map files. The page can ship with just engagement coverage now and extend gracefully as the sibling maps land.

## Open questions

* Standalone static page (e.g. `docs/kpi-coverage.html` regenerated by CI), or new tab in the existing Vite + React report UI? Standalone is simpler and link-shareable; UI integration reuses styling and could grow into a fuller "model coverage" surface over time. Decide at design-doc stage.
* Should the page be public-facing (committed to the repo, viewable on GitHub Pages) or local-only? toolUseResult.text:
{"id":"ABA-107","title":"KPI coverage page â€” at-a-glance HTML of every supported company + metric","description":"## What I want

A single HTML page I can open in a browser that shows, at a glance:

* Every ticker the model currently knows how to enrich with a company-specific KPI
* Which KPI family it falls under (user engagement / segment revenue / infrastructure bookings)
* What specific metric is being tracked (e.g. "Daily Active Uniques", "AWS segment revenue", "EUV net bookings")
* A link to the source filing the metric was last read from
* Which tickers are **deliberately excluded** and why (with the "revisit whenâ€¦" condition that would bring them back into scope)
* Last-checked status from the weekly drift CI â€” green / amber / red per ticker

The intent is one place I can send to someone (or check myself) to answer "does the model handle company X?" without grepping through JSON files.

## Why

Coverage is currently scattered across three JSON files (one per KPI family, only one of which exists today) plus the excluded-tickers block in each. To see the full picture you'd have to open each file and reason about it â€” fine for the maintainer, opaque to anyone else. A rendered page makes the scope visible and makes gaps obvious (e.g. "we cover 3 social companies and 0 cloud companies â€” that's the next gap to close").

It also turns the otherwise-invisible work (the excluded-tickers reasoning, the drift-CI signal, the source filings) into something visible and trustworthy.

## Source of truth

The page renders from the map files themselves â€” no separate copy of the data to maintain:

* `skills/_shared/engagement_kpi_map.json` (exists today, <issue id="32af2fee-a858-4aa2-8229-1620e8171f1f">ABA-66</issue>)
* `skills/_shared/segment_kpi_map.json` (created by <issue id="178bc70d-eda3-4e2a-956d-479c553aa7d6">ABA-65</issue>)
* `skills/_shared/bookings_kpi_map.json` (created by <issue id="d7f42e36-c909-4f2d-a494-aaf1afaf1685">ABA-67</issue>)
* Latest drift-CI state from the `engagement-kpi-drift` workflow run (and its siblings as they ship)

So the page is correct by construction â€” if a ticker is added to a map, it appears; if removed, it disappears. No documentation drift.

## What good looks like

* Open the page in a browser, see a table grouped by KPI family
* Each row: ticker, KPI name, last read value + period, source filing link, drift-CI status
* Below the main table, a "deliberately excluded" section with ticker + reason + revisit condition
* Footer shows when the page was generated and links to the milestone (M5.5) for context
* Page is static HTML â€” no server, no build step the user has to remember to run. Either committed to the repo regenerated by a GH Action, or rendered by the existing Vite + React report UI as a new tab.

## Out of scope

* Editing the maps from the page â€” read-only
* Historical view of "what KPIs were supported on date X" â€” current snapshot only
* Per-ticker drill-down with the full audit trail â€” the report JSON already does that

## Dependencies

* <issue id="32af2fee-a858-4aa2-8229-1620e8171f1f">ABA-66</issue> shipped (done) â€” engagement map exists
* For the page to show all three families: <issue id="178bc70d-eda3-4e2a-956d-479c553aa7d6">ABA-65</issue> and <issue id="d7f42e36-c909-4f2d-a494-aaf1afaf1685">ABA-67</issue> each need to land their map files. The page can ship with just engagement coverage now and extend gracefully as the sibling maps land.

## Open questions

* Standalone static page (e.g. `docs/kpi-coverage.html` regenerated by CI), or new tab in the existing Vite + React report UI? Standalone is simpler and link-shareable; UI integration reuses styling and could grow into a fuller "model coverage" surface over time. Decide at design-doc stage.
* Should the page be public-facing (committed to the repo, viewable on GitHub Pages) or local-only? Leaning public â€” it's effectively a feature catalogue and there's nothing sensitive in the map files.","projectMilestone":{"id":"b878941b-f9eb-4282-b524-bff4ee16be94","name":"M5.5 â€” Company-specific KPIs in modeling"},"priority":{"value":3,"name":"Medium"},"url":"https://linear.app/ababushkin/issue/ABA-107/kpi-coverage-page-at-a-glance-html-of-every-supported-company-metric","gitBranchName":"anton/aba-107-kpi-coverage-page-at-a-glance-html-of-every-supported","createdAt":"2026-05-17T03:20:32.515Z","updatedAt":"2026-05-17T03:20:32.515Z","archivedAt":null,"completedAt":null,"startedAt":null,"canceledAt":null,"dueDate":null,"slaStartedAt":null,"slaMediumRiskAt":null,"slaHighRiskAt":null,"slaBreachesAt":null,"slaType":"all","status":"Backlog","statusType":"backlog","labels":[],"attachments":[],"documents":[],"createdBy":"Anton Babushkin","createdById":"720d0535-dfc6-465f-8111-d1a3e6eab786","project":"Equity skill pack","projectId":"553fa5bd-f8cc-4a58-8236-2df12aa049d5","team":"Personal","teamId":"c20f56da-f121-4653-bf6c-dc1c8aac21c1"}

content.text:
{"id":"ABA-108","title":"Workflow for adding a ticker to a KPI map â€” interactive helper","description":"## What I want

A guided way to add a new ticker to any of the KPI maps that takes me from "I want to support company X" to a ready-to-commit pull request, without me having to remember every file that needs to move together.

Today, adding one ticker to the engagement KPI map is a 9-step process spread across 4 files (the map JSON, the changelog, the golden test regex bank, the test fixtures), with a couple of judgement calls in the middle (which press-release phrase to use, which regex to write). It works, but it's the kind of process where it's easy to forget step 7 and then wonder why the drift CI starts failing two weeks later.

## Why

Coverage growth is the single biggest lever for making the engagement-modifier feature useful â€” more tickers covered = more situations where the model actually adjusts. Right now the friction of adding a ticker is high enough that the universe will stay at three (Meta / Reddit / Pinterest) unless adding a fourth becomes routine. If I want this milestone to land its outcomes, the bottleneck I'll hit isn't the modifier itself â€” it's map maintenance.

This is also the difference between something only the original author can extend and something a future contributor (or future me, six months from now) can extend without re-reading the design doc.

## What I don't want this to be

This is **not** the auto-discovery skill (<issue id="bcc90f98-3d54-4836-846a-8611900851e5">ABA-105</issue>, deferred until the universe grows past \~8 tickers). That skill tries to be clever â€” classify the business model, propose a KPI mapping, validate it from filings. This ticket is the much smaller, much earlier piece: automate the mechanical parts of the current manual process, leave the judgement calls to a human.

## What good looks like

A single command â€” e.g. `python scripts/add_kpi_ticker.py TICKER --family engagement` â€” that:

1. **Fetches the latest 8-K Exhibit 99.1** for the ticker from EDGAR (or 6-K for foreign private issuers) and shows me the relevant section.
2. **Asks me the judgement calls** in a guided prompt â€” KPI name, long name, comparison basis (YoY/QoQ), signal type (growth/monetization), the source phrase the regex should anchor on. Reasonable defaults pre-filled where possible.
3. **Generates the JSON entry** for the right map file, **bumps** `schema_version`, and **appends a changelog entry** with the accession, filing date, and a one-line rationale I provide.
4. **Scaffolds the per-ticker regex** in the golden test module â€” pre-fills based on the source phrase I chose and a small library of known patterns ("X was N.NN billion / N% year-over-year"). I can tweak before committing.
5. **Adds a fixture row** to `press_release_snippets.json` using the snippet it just fetched, with the expected values I confirmed.
6. **Runs the golden tests and the drift driver locally** to confirm everything passes before I commit.
7. **Stages the changed files** for git commit and prints the suggested commit message.

End state: I run one command, answer 4â€“5 questions, run `git commit`, open a PR. The file-pair CI check and the golden tests already exist to catch anything the helper got wrong.

## Out of scope

* Removing or modifying existing entries â€” different shape, different risks. Add only.
* Working across all three KPI families with no per-family logic â€” segment revenue and infra bookings (when they exist) have different map shapes; the helper needs minimal per-family branching, but most code is shared.
* Auto-classifying which family a ticker belongs to â€” that's the discovery skill (<issue id="bcc90f98-3d54-4836-846a-8611900851e5">ABA-105</issue>). The user picks the family via `--family` flag.

## Dependencies

* <issue id="32af2fee-a858-4aa2-8229-1620e8171f1f">ABA-66</issue> shipped (done) â€” engagement map exists and is the first concrete consumer
* Optional but useful: EDGAR MCP server (currently soft-blocked) â€” would simplify the fetch step. Falls back to direct EDGAR HTTP otherwise, same as the modifier itself does.

## Open questions

* Live as a CLI script under `scripts/`, or as a slash-command skill (`/stock:kpi-add TICKER`)? Skill is more discoverable from inside Claude Code; script is portable and CI-callable. Decide at design-doc stage.
* Should it cover the excluded-tickers block too? Adding to `excluded_tickers` is a deliberately-different action â€” typically arises from "we tried to add this and it doesn't work" rather than "let's add this." Probably yes but with a separate `--exclude` flag. Lock at design-doc stage.

## How this relates to the auto-discovery skill (<issue id="bcc90f98-3d54-4836-846a-8611900851e5">ABA-105</issue>)

This ticket is the lightweight, manual-with-help version. <issue id="bcc90f98-3d54-4836-846a-8611900851e5">ABA-105</issue> is the heavyweight, automated-end-to-end version. If <issue id="bcc90f98-3d54-4836-846a-8611900851e5">ABA-105</issue> ever gets built, this ticket's helper becomes the fallback path for tickers the discovery skill can't classify. They coexist; they're not alternatives.","projectMilestone":{"id":"b878941b-f9eb-4282-b524-bff4ee16be94","name":"M5.5 â€” Company-specific KPIs in modeling"},"priority":{"value":3,"name":"Medium"},"url":"https://linear.app/ababushkin/issue/ABA-108/workflow-for-adding-a-ticker-to-a-kpi-map-interactive-helper","gitBranchName":"anton/aba-108-workflow-for-adding-a-ticker-to-a-kpi-map-interactive-helper","createdAt":"2026-05-17T03:21:52.009Z","updatedAt":"2026-05-17T03:21:52.009Z","archivedAt":null,"completedAt":null,"startedAt":null,"canceledAt":null,"dueDate":null,"slaStartedAt":null,"slaMediumRiskAt":null,"slaHighRiskAt":null,"slaBreachesAt":null,"slaType":"all","status":"Backlog","statusType":"backlog","labels":[],"attachments":[],"documents":[],"createdBy":"Anton Babushkin","createdById":"720d0535-dfc6-465f-8111-d1a3e6eab786","project":"Equity skill pack","projectId":"553fa5bd-f8cc-4a58-8236-2df12aa049d5","team":"Personal","teamId":"c20f56da-f121-4653-bf6c-dc1c8aac21c1"}

content.text:
{"id":"ABA-105","title":"/stock:kpi-discover â€” auto-propose engagement-KPI mappings for monitored tickers (v2 follow-on to ABA-66)","description":"## Context

<issue id="32af2fee-a858-4aa2-8229-1620e8171f1f">ABA-66</issue> ships the engagement-KPI modifier for `/stock:model` with a hard-coded KPI map (`skills/_shared/engagement_kpi_map.json`) for a 4-ticker seed set: META, NFLX, RDDT, GOOGL.

The hard-coded approach is right for v1 â€” small seed set, manual maintenance is cheaper than building a discovery system, and engagement-KPI names drift (META renamed DAUâ†’DAP in 2023; NFLX stopped quarterly sub disclosure in Q1 2025). Auto-discovery on a moving target is itself a hard problem.

**This issue is the v2 follow-on:** a dedicated skill that discovers and proposes KPI mappings for any ticker the user starts monitoring, so the map scales beyond the seed set without manual upkeep becoming the bottleneck.

## Trigger condition

File this work only when:

* <issue id="32af2fee-a858-4aa2-8229-1620e8171f1f">ABA-66</issue> has shipped successfully (Week 2 spike PROCEED and Week 3 build merged)
* The monitored-ticker universe has expanded to â‰¥8 APPLICATION/INCUMBENT names (or there's concrete evidence map maintenance is the constraint)

Until then this sits in the idea bank.

## Proposed shape (sketch â€” to be elaborated in a design doc when triggered)

`/stock:kpi-discover TICKER` â€” invoked when the user adds a new ticker to their monitored set:

1. Fetches the ticker's latest 8-K Exhibit 99.1 via EDGAR (same source contract as <issue id="32af2fee-a858-4aa2-8229-1620e8171f1f">ABA-66</issue>'s FR8).
2. Classifies the business model â€” ad-driven, subscription, marketplace, transaction, enterprise SaaS, hardware-OEM, infrastructure â€” from MD&A language + revenue-segment disclosures.
3. Proposes a primary + secondary KPI mapping based on the business-model class (a small meta-map: "ad-driven â†’ look for DAU/DAP/MAU + ARPU/CPM"; "subscription â†’ look for paid subs + ARM/ARPU").
4. Extracts the proposed KPIs from the 8-K to validate the proposal works on at least one quarter of real data.
5. Surfaces the proposal to the user (KPI name, value, period, source URL) for confirm/override.
6. On confirm: writes the entry into `engagement_kpi_map.json`, bumps `schema_version`, appends to the changelog.

## Why this is not v1 scope

* Discovery moves the manual-map problem rather than eliminating it â€” there's still a meta-map (business-model class â†’ candidate KPI families). The complexity is justified only when the universe is large enough that manual-per-ticker is the bottleneck.
* The hard-coded v1 has a weekly drift-CI check that will catch staleness within \~2 weeks; that mitigates the "META renames DAP" risk without needing discovery.
* Build-trap risk: building a discovery skill before the modifier itself has shown user value would be classic premature generalisation.

## Decomposition (when triggered)

This will need breaking into smaller tasks at design-doc time. Rough chunks:

1. Design doc + business-model classifier scope
2. Meta-map authoring (business-model class â†’ candidate KPI families)
3. EDGAR 8-K extraction reuse from <issue id="32af2fee-a858-4aa2-8229-1620e8171f1f">ABA-66</issue>
4. Proposal + MIP-confirm flow
5. Map-write + schema-version bump
6. Tests + golden fixtures

## Acceptance criteria

To be defined at design-doc time. Stub:

* Running `/stock:kpi-discover SPOT` (or another ticker not in v1 seed) produces a confirmed KPI-map entry within 60s of user invocation
* Existing v1 entries are preserved; only new entries are added
* Business-model classifier has â‰¥80% precision on a labelled 10-ticker validation set

## References

* Parent: <issue id="32af2fee-a858-4aa2-8229-1620e8171f1f">ABA-66</issue> â€” engagement KPI enrichment (v1, hard-coded)
* Design doc: `docs/design-docs/engagement-kpi-enrichment/design-doc.md` â€” see OQ7 in Open Questions for the motivation
* Source contract: <issue id="32af2fee-a858-4aa2-8229-1620e8171f1f">ABA-66</issue> FR8 (EDGAR 8-K Exhibit 99.1) â€” reused here","projectMilestone":{"id":"b878941b-f9eb-4282-b524-bff4ee16be94","name":"M5.5 â€” Company-specific KPIs in modeling"},"priority":{"value":4,"name":"Low"},"url":"https://linear.app/ababushkin/issue/ABA-105/stockkpi-discover-auto-propose-engagement-kpi-mappings-for-monitored","gitBranchName":"anton/aba-105-stockkpi-discover-auto-propose-engagement-kpi-mappings-for","createdAt":"2026-05-13T15:26:34.994Z","updatedAt":"2026-05-17T03:21:52.009Z","archivedAt":null,"completedAt":null,"startedAt":null,"canceledAt":null,"dueDate":null,"slaStartedAt":null,"slaMediumRiskAt":null,"slaHighRiskAt":null,"slaBreachesAt":null,"slaType":"all","status":"Backlog","statusType":"backlog","labels":[],"attachments":[],"documents":[],"createdBy":"Anton Babushkin","createdById":"720d0535-dfc6-465f-8111-d1a3e6eab786","project":"Equity skill pack","projectId":"553fa5bd-f8cc-4a58-8236-2df12aa049d5","team":"Personal","teamId":"c20f56da-f121-4653-bf6c-dc1c8aac21c1"}

This will need breaking into smaller tasks at design-doc time. Rough chunks:

1. Design doc + business-model classifier scope
2. Meta-map authoring (business-model class â†’ candidate KPI families)
3. EDGAR 8-K extraction reuse from <issue id="32af2fee-a858-4aa2-8229-1620e8171f1f">ABA-66</issue>
4. Proposal + MIP-confirm flow
5. Map-write + schema-version bump
6. Tests + golden fixtures

## Acceptance criteria

To be defined at design-doc time. Stub:

* Running `/stock:kpi-discover SPOT` (or another ticker not in v1 seed) produces a confirmed KPI-map entry within 60s of user invocation
* Existing v1 entries are preserved; only new entries are added
* Business-model classifier has â‰¥80% precision on a labelled 10-ticker validation set

## References

* Parent: <issue id="32af2fee-a858-4aa2-8229-1620e8171f1f">ABA-66</issue> â€” engagement KPI enrichment (v1, hard-coded)
* Design doc: `docs/design-docs/engagement-kpi-enrichment/design-doc.md` â€” see OQ7 in Open Questions for the motivation

{"id":"ABA-103","title":"Spike: auto-derive base WACC for /stock:model instead of always asking","description":"## Problem

`/stock:model` currently fires the Manual Input Protocol for **base WACC** on every ESTABLISHED run because yfinance doesn't expose the inputs needed to derive it (risk-free rate, beta, capital structure, ERP). The user has to type the number in by hand every time â€” a real ergonomics drag, and the value they end up using comes from external lookup anyway (e.g. [alphaspread.com](<http://alphaspread.com>) per the conversation that triggered this ticket).

## Spike goal

Decide **how /stock:model should obtain base WACC**, without committing to an implementation. Output is a written recommendation, not code.

## Questions to answer

1. **What data source(s) are viable?** Options to evaluate include â€” but are not limited to:
   * Third-party WACC aggregators (alphaspread, gurufocus, [simplywall.st](<http://simplywall.st>), finbox, etc.) â€” what are the ToS, scrape stability, accuracy claims, latency?
   * Compute from primitives: risk-free rate (FRED 10y Treasury or equivalent), beta (yfinance already exposes), ERP (Damodaran's annual update, or a fixed assumption), debt weight + cost of debt (yfinance financials).
   * Existing MCP servers / open APIs we haven't surveyed.
2. **What's the right interaction model?**
   * Auto-fetch and use silently (no MIP) â€” confidence implications?
   * Pre-fill the MIP with the fetched value as a suggestion, user confirms or overrides (matches the SBC dilution pre-fill pattern in the pre-profit variant).
   * Show the fetched value alongside the derivation (risk-free + beta أ— ERP), so the user can sanity-check.
3. **How do we handle source failure?** Scrapes break; APIs rate-limit. Should the fallback always be "ask the user"?
4. **Does the chosen approach generalise to other always-asked inputs?** Notably terminal FCF margin target and base exit multiple in the pre-profit variant â€” both currently MIP-only.

## Out of scope for the spike

* Building any of the above. The spike output is a recommendation + a follow-up ticket to implement the chosen path.
* Replacing the MIP framework itself.

## Acceptance

A written recommendation in the ticket comments naming:

* The preferred data source (with reasoning, including rejected alternatives)
* The preferred interaction model (auto / pre-fill / show derivation)
* The failure-mode fallback
* A follow-up implementation ticket filed against the chosen path

## Context

Triggered by a /stock:model NVDA run on 2026-05-14 where the user looked up WACC on [alphaspread.com](<http://alphaspread.com>) and asked whether to wire it in. Conversation noted alphaspread as one viable source but explicitly asked the spike not to pre-commit to it.","priority":{"value":4,"name":"Low"},"url":"https://linear.app/ababushkin/issue/ABA-103/spike-auto-derive-base-wacc-for-stockmodel-instead-of-always-asking","gitBranchName":"anton/aba-103-spike-auto-derive-base-wacc-for-stockmodel-instead-of-always","createdAt":"2026-05-13T15:03:07.710Z","updatedAt":"2026-05-13T15:07:17.704Z","archivedAt":null,"completedAt":null,"startedAt":null,"canceledAt":null,"dueDate":null,"slaStartedAt":null,"slaMediumRiskAt":null,"slaHighRiskAt":null,"slaBreachesAt":null,"slaType":"all","status":"Backlog","statusType":"backlog","labels":[],"attachments":[],"documents":[],"createdBy":"Anton Babushkin","createdById":"720d0535-dfc6-465f-8111-d1a3e6eab786","project":"Equity skill pack","projectId":"553fa5bd-f8cc-4a58-8236-2df12aa049d5","team":"Personal","teamId":"c20f56da-f121-4653-bf6c-dc1c8aac21c1"}

{"id":"ABA-109","title":"Drift CI: switch weekly run to live EDGAR + Yahoo fetches","description":"## What I want

The weekly drift-check workflow (`.github/workflows/engagement-kpi-drift.yml`) should actually fetch the latest 8-K from SEC and the live Yahoo `/analysis/` page for each supported ticker, rather than running against the committed test fixtures.

## Why this matters

The drift check exists to catch upstream format changes â€” a publisher renaming a metric (Meta did DAU â†’ DAP in 2023), Yahoo restructuring the EPS Trend column, SEC changing the press-release filing convention. **The current implementation can't actually catch any of those things**, because it runs against frozen committed snippets. If the live Meta press release changed its wording tomorrow, the drift CI would stay green â€” it would only fail if someone changed our test fixtures or our regex.

This is a known limitation, captured deliberately at build-time: hermetic-against-fixtures was the right starting shape (fast, free, deterministic, no flaky network failures on weekend cron runs). But it means the workflow is currently only validating that "the code still parses the test data," not "the live publisher data still matches our assumptions" â€” and the latter is the whole point.

## What good looks like

The weekly scheduled run does the real thing:

1. **Fetches the latest 8-K Ex 99.1** from EDGAR per ticker (using the same source-resolution order the modifier itself uses: EDGAR MCP â†’ direct HTTP â†’ fallback).
2. **Fetches Yahoo's live** `/analysis/` page for each ticker and reads the current EPS Trend table.
3. **Runs the same extractors** against the live content (not the fixtures).
4. **Reports per-ticker status** and trips the 2-consecutive-failure gate as it does today.

The pull-request mode and the `workflow_dispatch` test path keep using fixtures â€” those need to stay hermetic so CI on every PR doesn't depend on SEC being up.

## What needs handling

* **Rate limits.** SEC requires a custom User-Agent and is reasonable about request volume; Yahoo is more aggressive. Need to space the requests and handle transient failures cleanly (which is exactly what the 2-consecutive-failure rule is designed for â€” one transient fetch failure shouldn't trip the gate).
* **Drift cause attribution.** When a ticker flips to non-applied, the report should distinguish "EDGAR fetch failed" from "EDGAR returned content but regex didn't match" from "Yahoo page structure changed." That's what makes the alert actionable.
* **Cost.** Live fetches mean every weekly run incurs network calls. For 3 tickers this is negligible; worth noting if the ticker universe ever grows past 20.

## Out of scope

* The PR-triggered file-pair check stays as-is â€” it's a git-diff check, no fetches involved.
* The forward-log accumulation (NFR7 backtest substitute) is separate from this work â€” that's driven by real `/stock:model` invocations, not drift CI.

## Dependencies

* The modifier's live-fetch code path (built in <issue id="32af2fee-a858-4aa2-8229-1620e8171f1f">ABA-66</issue>) is the same code this would call. Reusable as-is.
* No new tooling needed.

## How this connects to the other M5.5 work

When <issue id="178bc70d-eda3-4e2a-956d-479c553aa7d6">ABA-65</issue> (segment revenue) and <issue id="d7f42e36-c909-4f2d-a494-aaf1afaf1685">ABA-67</issue> (infra bookings) ship, each gets its own drift CI workflow on the same pattern. Doing the live-fetch upgrade once on the engagement workflow gives us the template the others copy from â€” so this is worth doing before the sibling workflows are built rather than after.","projectMilestone":{"id":"b878941b-f9eb-4282-b524-bff4ee16be94","name":"M5.5 â€” Company-specific KPIs in modeling"},"priority":{"value":3,"name":"Medium"},"url":"https://linear.app/ababushkin/issue/ABA-109/drift-ci-switch-weekly-run-to-live-edgar-yahoo-fetches","gitBranchName":"anton/aba-109-drift-ci-switch-weekly-run-to-live-edgar-yahoo-fetches","createdAt":"2026-05-17T03:56:33.220Z","updatedAt":"2026-05-17T03:56:33.220Z","archivedAt":null,"completedAt":null,"startedAt":null,"canceledAt":null,"dueDate":null,"slaStartedAt":null,"slaMediumRiskAt":null,"slaHighRiskAt":null,"slaBreachesAt":null,"slaType":"all","status":"Backlog","statusType":"backlog","labels":[],"attachments":[],"documents":[],"createdBy":"Anton Babushkin","createdById":"720d0535-dfc6-465f-8111-d1a3e6eab786","project":"Equity skill pack","projectId":"553fa5bd-f8cc-4a58-8236-2df12aa049d5","team":"Personal","teamId":"c20f56da-f121-4653-bf6c-dc1c8aac21c1"}

content.text:
{"id":"ABA-113","title":"Diagram + ADR for the /stock:signal override evaluation ladder","description":"## Why

The signal skill has a growing override ladder â€” Override 1 (pre-profit base effect), Override 2 (transition year), Override 2.5 (SBC-distorted earnings, added in PR #2), Override 3 (qualitative failure), Override 4 (yield trap). The rules are evaluated in a specific order, and that order is load-bearing â€” for example, Override 2.5 has to fire *before* Override 3 so it can set `Qualitative = FLAG` before Override 3 reads that field.

Right now the evaluation order lives in prose inside `skills/signal/SKILL.md` آ§OVERRIDE only. No diagram, no precedence table, no ADR. PR #2's "Override 2.5" naming came out of exactly that gap â€” we wedged in a decimal to avoid renumbering every existing reference. The next override insertion will hit the same problem.

## What good looks like

A small artefact that lets any future reader â€” human or agent â€” answer three questions without re-reading the whole skill:

1. In what order do the overrides fire, and which fields does each one read vs. write?
2. Which combinations short-circuit which (e.g. Override 2.5 â†’ Qualitative = FLAG pre-empts Override 3)?
3. Where would I insert a hypothetical Override 5, and what would I have to be careful about?

## Suggested shape

* ADR at `docs/adrs/signal-override-precedence.md` capturing:
  * the ordered list of overrides + their fire-conditions
  * which fields each override reads and writes
  * the pre-emption rules between adjacent overrides
  * one worked example per override showing input â†’ output
* A Mermaid flowchart embedded in the ADR (renders in GitHub + Linear) and re-linked from `skills/signal/SKILL.md` آ§OVERRIDE
* Optional: a precedence table at the top of آ§OVERRIDE that mirrors the ADR for quick lookup

## Out of scope

* Renaming overrides off the numeric scheme (separate, larger change)
* Adding or modifying any override behaviour â€” this is documentation-only
* The same exercise for `/stock:model` overrides (separate ticket if/when warranted)

## Triggered by

PR #2 â€” Override 2.5 was inserted as a decimal specifically because the precedence wasn't documented anywhere a casual reader could find it. [https://github.com/ababushkin/agentic-equity-research-skills/pull/2](<https://github.com/ababushkin/agentic-equity-research-skills/pull/2>)","priority":{"value":3,"name":"Medium"},"url":"https://linear.app/ababushkin/issue/ABA-113/diagram-adr-for-the-stocksignal-override-evaluation-ladder","gitBranchName":"anton/aba-113-diagram-adr-for-the-stocksignal-override-evaluation-ladder","createdAt":"2026-05-17T04:34:34.376Z","updatedAt":"2026-05-17T07:34:19.534Z","archivedAt":null,"completedAt":null,"startedAt":null,"canceledAt":null,"dueDate":null,"slaStartedAt":null,"slaMediumRiskAt":null,"slaHighRiskAt":null,"slaBreachesAt":null,"slaType":"all","status":"Backlog","statusType":"backlog","labels":[],"attachments":[],"documents":[],"createdBy":"Anton Babushkin","createdById":"720d0535-dfc6-465f-8111-d1a3e6eab786","project":"Equity skill pack","projectId":"553fa5bd-f8cc-4a58-8236-2df12aa049d5","team":"Personal","teamId":"c20f56da-f121-4653-bf6c-dc1c8aac21c1"}

content.text:
{"id":"ABA-95","title":"M5 Model skill: optional structure parity with Signal skill","description":"Low-severity follow-ups from the <issue id="7f414a0c-d9b9-4f9b-a19b-71787563402d">ABA-61</issue> skill-creator audit. Neither blocks correctness; both would improve readability and consistency.

1. **No top-level Input Schema table.** `skills/signal/SKILL.md` has `## 3. Input Schema` as a table summarising every field, source, and data-gap handling. `skills/model/SKILL.md` embeds the same information inline in the GATHER sections (ESTABLISHED and EMERGING). A top-level table would let a reader see at a glance what MCP calls are needed and which fields are required vs optional.
2. **No top-level Methodology section.** Signal has `## 2. Methodology` summarising GARP, PEG, P/S, Rule of 40, AI layer. Model jumps from Identity straight to `## 2. Why a context gate`. The DCF math is described in COMPUTE, but a one-paragraph methodology overview (two-stage DCF for ESTABLISHED, revenue-multiple exit + FCF inflection for EMERGING, source references) would mirror the Signal pattern.

Both are nice-to-have polish, not defects. **Structure:**

1. **Hero** â€” one-line description + badge (Python, Claude Code)
2. **What it does** â€” 3-bullet summary of screen â†’ signal â†’ model chain; who it's for
3. **Example output** â€” copy-pasted terminal output from a real `/screen NVDA` run (not a screenshot â€” text so it's indexable and copy-pasteable)
4. **Architecture** â€” 2-paragraph prose (MCP servers + Claude Code skills, no diagram needed for v1)
5. **Prerequisites** â€” Claude Code + Python 3.11+ + Anthropic subscription
6. **Quick start** â€” exactly 3 steps: clone, `./setup.sh`, run `/screen NVDA` in Claude Code
7. **Skills reference** â€” table of `/screen`, `/signal`, `/timing`, `/model`, `/equity` with one-line descriptions
8. **MCP tools reference** â€” table of yf and edgar tools with one-line descriptions

**Acceptance criteria:**

* Someone unfamiliar with the project can understand what it produces from the README alone, without running it
* The example output is real (actually run against a ticker, pasted in)
* Quick start takes â‰¤3 steps","projectMilestone":{"id":"35d9dcfb-6e3a-473f-9cd3-d84fdd70beb5","name":"M8 â€” Open Source Release"},"priority":{"value":3,"name":"Medium"},"url":"https://linear.app/ababushkin/issue/ABA-50/readme-hero-section-example-output-quick-start","gitBranchName":"anton/aba-50-readme-hero-section-example-output-quick-start","createdAt":"2026-05-12T11:17:51.654Z","updatedAt":"2026-05-17T04:17:55.414Z","archivedAt":null,"completedAt":null,"startedAt":null,"canceledAt":null,"dueDate":null,"slaStartedAt":null,"slaMediumRiskAt":null,"slaHighRiskAt":null,"slaBreachesAt":null,"slaType":"all","status":"Backlog","statusType":"backlog","labels":[],"attachments":[],"documents":[],"createdBy":"Anton Babushkin","createdById":"720d0535-dfc6-465f-8111-d1a3e6eab786","project":"Equity skill pack","projectId":"553fa5bd-f8cc-4a58-8236-2df12aa049d5","team":"Personal","teamId":"c20f56da-f121-4653-bf6c-dc1c8aac21c1"}

content.text:
{"id":"ABA-51","title":"setup.sh â€” one-command install (venv + pip + MCP config)","description":"Single script that takes a fresh clone to a working installation. Every manual step in the current setup is a dropout point.

**What it must do:**

1. Create a Python venv in `mcp/yf/.venv` and `mcp/edgar/.venv` (or a shared root venv)
2. `pip install -r requirements.txt` for both MCP servers
3. Detect Claude Code's MCP config location (`~/.claude/mcp.json` or project `.mcp.json`) and inject the yf and edgar server entries if not already present â€” or print the exact JSON block to paste if auto-inject is too risky
4. Copy `.env.example` â†’ `.env` if `.env` doesn't exist yet
5. Print a success message with the exact command to run first: `/screen NVDA`

**Failure handling:**

* If Python < 3.11, print a clear error and exit
* If Claude Code not found, print install link and continue (MCP config can be done manually)
* Idempotent: safe to run twice

**Acceptance criteria:**

* Running `./setup.sh` on a clean clone with Python 3.11+ and Claude Code installed produces a working setup with no additional manual steps
* Script exits non-zero on any failure with a human-readable error message","projectMilestone":{"id":"35d9dcfb-6e3a-473f-9cd3-d84fdd70beb5","name":"M8 â€” Open Source Release"},"priority":{"value":3,"name":"Medium"},"url":"https://linear.app/ababushkin/issue/ABA-51/setupsh-one-command-install-venv-pip-mcp-config","gitBranchName":"anton/aba-51-setupsh-one-command-install-venv-pip-mcp-config","createdAt":"2026-05-12T11:17:57.873Z","updatedAt":"2026-05-17T04:17:59.665Z","archivedAt":null,"completedAt":null,"startedAt":null,"canceledAt":null,"dueDate":null,"slaStartedAt":null,"slaMediumRiskAt":null,"slaHighRiskAt":null,"slaBreachesAt":null,"slaType":"all","status":"Backlog","statusType":"backlog","labels":[],"attachments":[],"documents":[],"createdBy":"Anton Babushkin","createdById":"720d0535-dfc6-465f-8111-d1a3e6eab786","project":"Equity skill pack","projectId":"553fa5bd-f8cc-4a58-8236-2df12aa049d5","team":"Personal","teamId":"c20f56da-f121-4653-bf6c-dc1c8aac21c1"}

content.text:
{"id":"ABA-52","title":"Scrub personal data from reports/ before going public","description":"Audit and clean the repo before making it public.

**Steps:**

1. Review all files in `reports/` â€” remove any real report JSONs that shouldn't be public; add `reports/*.json` to `.gitignore` (keep `reports/.gitkeep`)
2. Check git history for any accidentally committed sensitive data (`git log --all --full-history -- reports/`)
3. Review `mcp/yf/test_tools.py` and `mcp/edgar/test_tools.py` for any hardcoded personal tickers or account details
4. Check `.mcp.json` for any local paths or secrets that shouldn't be in the repo
5. Confirm `.env` (if it exists) is in `.gitignore`

**Acceptance criteria:**

* `git log --all -- reports/` shows no real report data
* `.gitignore` covers `reports/*.json`, `.env`, `**/.venv/`, `**/__pycache__/`
* No personal paths or credentials in any tracked file","projectMilestone":{"id":"35d9dcfb-6e3a-473f-9cd3-d84fdd70beb5","name":"M8 â€” Open Source Release"},"priority":{"value":3,"name":"Medium"},"url":"https://linear.app/ababushkin/issue/ABA-52/scrub-personal-data-from-reports-before-going-public","gitBranchName":"anton/aba-52-scrub-personal-data-from-reports-before-going-public","createdAt":"2026-05-12T11:18:00.462Z","updatedAt":"2026-05-17T04:17:57.374Z","archivedAt":null,"completedAt":null,"startedAt":null,"canceledAt":null,"dueDate":null,"slaStartedAt":null,"slaMediumRiskAt":null,"slaHighRiskAt":null,"slaBreachesAt":null,"slaType":"all","status":"Backlog","statusType":"backlog","labels":[],"attachments":[],"documents":[],"createdBy":"Anton Babushkin","createdById":"720d0535-dfc6-465f-8111-d1a3e6eab786","project":"Equity skill pack","projectId":"553fa5bd-f8cc-4a58-8236-2df12aa049d5","team":"Personal","teamId":"c20f56da-f121-4653-bf6c-dc1c8aac21c1"}

{"id":"ABA-53","title":"Example outputs â€” committed sample runs for NVDA and one pre-profit ticker","description":"Shareable, pre-run outputs that let people evaluate the tool before installing it. The key insight from distribution strategy: people share outputs, not GitHub links.

**Deliverables:**

* `examples/screen_NVDA.json` â€” real `/screen` output for NVDA (established, profitable)
* `examples/screen_PLTR.json` â€” real `/screen` output for PLTR (emerging, pre-profit) â€” shows the two-variant logic
* `examples/signal_NVDA.json` â€” real `/signal` output for NVDA
* `examples/README.md` â€” one paragraph explaining these are real runs, not curated; links back to main README

**Why two tickers:** NVDA is the obvious demo; PLTR is controversial enough that people will have opinions and share/argue about the output, which drives distribution.

**Acceptance criteria:**

* All JSON files are real outputs (not mocked), committed to `examples/`
* Dates in the JSON reflect when the run was done
* Main README references `examples/` with a link","projectMilestone":{"id":"35d9dcfb-6e3a-473f-9cd3-d84fdd70beb5","name":"M8 â€” Open Source Release"},"priority":{"value":3,"name":"Medium"},"url":"https://linear.app/ababushkin/issue/ABA-53/example-outputs-committed-sample-runs-for-nvda-and-one-pre-profit","gitBranchName":"anton/aba-53-example-outputs-committed-sample-runs-for-nvda-and-one-pre","createdAt":"2026-05-12T11:18:03.149Z","updatedAt":"2026-05-17T09:50:55.577Z","archivedAt":null,"completedAt":null,"startedAt":null,"canceledAt":null,"dueDate":null,"slaStartedAt":null,"slaMediumRiskAt":null,"slaHighRiskAt":null,"slaBreachesAt":null,"slaType":"all","status":"Backlog","statusType":"backlog","labels":[],"attachments":[],"documents":[],"createdBy":"Anton Babushkin","createdById":"720d0535-dfc6-465f-8111-d1a3e6eab786","project":"Equity skill pack","projectId":"553fa5bd-f8cc-4a58-8236-2df12aa049d5","team":"Personal","teamId":"c20f56da-f121-4653-bf6c-dc1c8aac21c1"}

{"id":"ABA-54","title":"GitHub Actions CI â€” run MCP server tests on push","description":"Basic CI that gives the project a green badge and catches MCP server regressions on push.

**Workflow:**

* Trigger: push to `main`, PRs to `main`
* Matrix: Python 3.11 and 3.12
* Steps: checkout â†’ setup Python â†’ install deps (both MCP servers) â†’ run `python -m pytest mcp/yf/test_tools.py mcp/edgar/test_tools.py -v`
* Badge in README: `![CI](https://github.com/OWNER/REPO/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg)`

**Note:** Tests that hit live yfinance/EDGAR APIs should be marked to skip in CI if they require network (check existing test structure first â€” may already have fixtures or mocking).

**Acceptance criteria:**

* Green CI badge visible on GitHub
* Push to main triggers the workflow and it passes
* Workflow file at `.github/workflows/ci.yml`","projectMilestone":{"id":"35d9dcfb-6e3a-473f-9cd3-d84fdd70beb5","name":"M8 â€” Open Source Release"},"priority":{"value":3,"name":"Medium"},"url":"https://linear.app/ababushkin/issue/ABA-54/github-actions-ci-run-mcp-server-tests-on-push","gitBranchName":"anton/aba-54-github-actions-ci-run-mcp-server-tests-on-push","createdAt":"2026-05-12T11:18:05.711Z","updatedAt":"2026-05-17T09:50:57.147Z","archivedAt":null,"completedAt":null,"startedAt":null,"canceledAt":null,"dueDate":null,"slaStartedAt":null,"slaMediumRiskAt":null,"slaHighRiskAt":null,"slaBreachesAt":null,"slaType":"all","status":"Backlog","statusType":"backlog","labels":[],"attachments":[],"documents":[],"createdBy":"Anton Babushkin","createdById":"720d0535-dfc6-465f-8111-d1a3e6eab786","project":"Equity skill pack","projectId":"553fa5bd-f8cc-4a58-8236-2df12aa049d5","team":"Personal","teamId":"c20f56da-f121-4653-bf6c-dc1c8aac21c1"}

content.text:
{"id":"ABA-55","title":".env.example â€” document all environment variables","description":"Single source of truth for what environment variables the project requires or supports.

**Steps:**

1. Audit both MCP servers (`mcp/yf/` and `mcp/edgar/`) for any `os.environ` or `os.getenv` calls
2. For each var: document name, purpose, required/optional, example value
3. Write `.env.example` with all vars commented with descriptions
4. Confirm `.env` is in `.gitignore`

**Expected vars (verify against code):**

* Likely none required for yf (yfinance is unauthenticated)
* EDGAR: possibly a user-agent string for SEC rate limiting
* Any debug/logging flags

**Acceptance criteria:**

* `.env.example` committed and covers every env var the servers read
* `.env` is in `.gitignore`

{"id":"ABA-55","title":".env.example â€” document all environment variables","description":"Single source of truth for what environment variables the project requires or supports.

**Steps:**

1. Audit both MCP servers (`mcp/yf/` and `mcp/edgar/`) for any `os.environ` or `os.getenv` calls
2. For each var: document name, purpose, required/optional, example value
3. Write `.env.example` with all vars commented with descriptions
4. Confirm `.env` is in `.gitignore`

**Expected vars (verify against code):**

* Likely none required for yf (yfinance is unauthenticated)
* EDGAR: possibly a user-agent string for SEC rate limiting
* Any debug/logging flags

**Acceptance criteria:**

* `.env.example` committed and covers every env var the servers read
* `.env` is in `.gitignore`
* `setup.sh` copies `.env.example` â†’ `.env` if `.env` doesn't exist","projectMilestone":{"id":"35d9dcfb-6e3a-473f-9cd3-d84fdd70beb5","name":"M8 â€” Open Source Release"},"priority":{"value":3,"name":"Medium"},"url":"https://linear.app/ababushkin/issue/ABA-55/envexample-document-all-environment-variables","gitBranchName":"anton/aba-55-envexample-document-all-environment-variables","createdAt":"2026-05-12T11:18:10.995Z","updatedAt":"2026-05-17T04:18:06.655Z","archivedAt":null,"completedAt":null,"startedAt":null,"canceledAt":null,"dueDate":null,"slaStartedAt":null,"slaMediumRiskAt":null,"slaHighRiskAt":null,"slaBreachesAt":null,"slaType":"all","status":"Backlog","statusType":"backlog","labels":[],"attachments":[],"documents":[],"createdBy":"Anton Babushkin","createdById":"720d0535-dfc6-465f-8111-d1a3e6eab786","project":"Equity skill pack","projectId":"553fa5bd-f8cc-4a58-8236-2df12aa049d5","team":"Personal","teamId":"c20f56da-f121-4653-bf6c-dc1c8aac21c1"}

{"id":"ABA-56","title":"CONTRIBUTING.md â€” how to add a skill or MCP tool","description":"Short guide for contributors. Keeps it to the two most likely contribution types.

**Sections:**

1. **Adding a skill** â€” where skill files live (`skills/`), the required structure (link to SPEC.md), how to register in Claude Code
2. **Adding an MCP tool** â€” where tool definitions live (`mcp/yf/tools.py` or `mcp/edgar/tools.py`), the tool schema pattern, how to add a test in `test_tools.py`
3. **Running tests locally** â€” one code block showing the pytest command
4. **PR checklist** â€” tests pass, skill file follows SPEC.md conventions, example output updated if the JSON schema changes

**Keep it short.** CONTRIBUTING.md that nobody reads is worse than none â€” aim for under 2 pages.

**Acceptance criteria:**

* A contributor can add a new yfinance tool following only CONTRIBUTING.md

content.text:
{"id":"ABA-57","title":"Add LICENSE file","description":"Add a LICENSE file to the repo root before going public. Without one, the project is technically all-rights-reserved by default regardless of being on GitHub.

**Decision: MIT License** â€” permissive, widely understood, no friction for users who want to build on top of this. Appropriate for a personal tool/skill-pack with no commercial restrictions intended.

**Steps:**

1. Create `LICENSE` in the repo root with the MIT license text, year 2026, and your name
2. Reference the license in the README (one line: "MIT License")

**Acceptance criteria:**

* `LICENSE` file present in repo root
* GitHub picks it up and displays the license badge on the repo page","projectMilestone":{"id":"35d9dcfb-6e3a-473f-9cd3-d84fdd70beb5","name":"M8 â€” Open Source Release"},"priority":{"value":3,"name":"Medium"},"url":"https://linear.app/ababushkin/issue/ABA-57/add-license-file","gitBranchName":"anton/aba-57-add-license-file","createdAt":"2026-05-12T11:19:49.220Z","updatedAt":"2026-05-17T04:17:53.835Z","archivedAt":null,"completedAt":null,"startedAt":null,"canceledAt":null,"dueDate":null,"slaStartedAt":null,"slaMediumRiskAt":null,"slaHighRiskAt":null,"slaBreachesAt":null,"slaType":"all","status":"Backlog","statusType":"backlog","labels":[],"attachments":[],"documents":[],"createdBy":"Anton Babushkin","createdById":"720d0535-dfc6-465f-8111-d1a3e6eab786","project":"Equity skill pack","projectId":"553fa5bd-f8cc-4a58-8236-2df12aa049d5","team":"Personal","teamId":"c20f56da-f121-4653-bf6c-dc1c8aac21c1"}

# AGENTS.md

Instructions for any coding agent (Claude Code, Codex, etc.) working in this repository. Tool-agnostic by design â€” see `CLAUDE.md` for any Claude-Code-specific additions.

## What this repo is

A personal equity research skill-pack for a tech-focused investor. See `CHARTER.md` for what this pack is for and which boundaries are locked, and `DESIGN.md` for the architectural reference.

Tasks and milestones live in Linear: https://linear.app/ababushkin/project/equity-skill-pack-b8446cbaab6b/overview

## Operating principle â€” pointer

This pack optimises for **depth on a curated set of specialist-supported tickers** (see `CHARTER.md` â†’ *Operating Principle â€” Depth over Breadth* for the full statement, and `COVERAGE.md` for the seven currently-supported names plus the contribution path for adding more). Generic improvements are deprioritised vs ticker-specific depth, except correctness fixes (ABA-110 SBC strip, ABA-111 growth-rate ceiling) which are P0.

**Until ABA-110 and ABA-111 land**, no `/stock-model` report should be treated as decision-grade â€” IVs are inflated by un-stripped SBC and trough-extrapolated FCF growth; position-sizing recommendations should be discounted.

Playbook layer (`playbooks/TICKER.md` loaded by `/stock-model` for covered tickers): spec lives in `DESIGN.md` â†’ *Playbook Layer*; implementation tracked in ABA-112.

## Build tools by component

| Component | Build with |
|-----------|-----------|
| yfinance MCP server | `/pde:design-doc` â†’ `agent-skills:build` |
| EDGAR MCP server | `/pde:design-doc` â†’ `agent-skills:build` |
| `/stock-signal`, `/stock-screen`, `/stock-timing`, `/stock-model`, `/stock-equity` skills | `skill-creator:skill-creator` |
| Report UI (Vite + React) | `agent-skills:build` |

## Commands

```bash
# UI
cd ui && npm run dev        # localhost:5173
cd ui && npm run build

# MCP servers
cd mcp/yf && python server.py
cd mcp/edgar && python server.py
```

## Permissions

Per-agent local settings pre-allow `rtk ls *` and `rtk read *` â€” use these instead of bare `ls`/`cat`.

- Claude Code: `.claude/settings.local.json`
- Codex: `.codex/settings.local.json`

## Linear workflow

When working on a Linear issue:
1. **On start** â€” set the issue status to **In Progress** using `mcp__linear-server__save_issue`
2. **On complete** â€” set the issue status to **Done** using `mcp__linear-server__save_issue` only after changes have been committed and pushed

Always update Linear status. Do not leave issues in the wrong state.

## Linear wave promotion

M5 work is grouped into waves via Linear labels (`wave-1`, `wave-1.5-gate`, `wave-2`, `wave-3`, `wave-4`) and gated via `blockedBy` relationships. When a Linear issue moves to Done:

1. Run a 30-second smoke check on the just-completed work (eyeball one representative output). If it fails, the work isn't actually done â€” reopen and stop.
2. Find issues whose `blockedBy` list contained the just-completed issue â€” these are now unblocked candidates.
3. Filter unblocked candidates by wave label â€” promote in wave order; never skip a wave.
4. Promote 1â€“3 unblocked candidates to Todo, keeping the Todo queue â‰¤ 3 deep.
5. Linear's blockers enforce the hard stops (Wave 1 â†’ ABA-82 gate â†’ Wave 2 â†’ Wave 3 â†’ Wave 4); never manually bypass them.

## Git

Do not add `Co-Authored-By` trailers to commit messages.

# AGENTS.md

Instructions for any coding agent (Claude Code, Codex, etc.) working in this repository. Tool-agnostic by design â€” see `CLAUDE.md` for any Claude-Code-specific additions.

## What this repo is

A personal equity research skill-pack for a tech-focused investor. See `CHARTER.md` for what this pack is for and which boundaries are locked, and `DESIGN.md` for the architectural reference.

Tasks and milestones live in Linear: https://linear.app/ababushkin/project/equity-skill-pack-b8446cbaab6b/overview

## Operating principle â€” pointer

This pack optimises for **depth on a curated set of specialist-supported tickers** (see `CHARTER.md` â†’ *Operating Principle â€” Depth over Breadth* for the full statement, and `COVERAGE.md` for the seven currently-supported names plus the contribution path for adding more). Generic improvements are deprioritised vs ticker-specific depth.

Correctness baseline (as of 2026-05-17): ABA-110 (SBC strip) and ABA-111 (growth-rate ceiling) are landed â€” `/stock-model` outputs are decision-grade on the correctness axis. Open follow-up: ABA-104 base-year FCF spike.

Playbook layer (`playbooks/TICKER.md` loaded by `/stock-model` for covered tickers): spec lives in `DESIGN.md` â†’ *Playbook Layer*; implementation tracked in ABA-112.

## Build tools by component

| Component | Build with |
|-----------|-----------|
| yfinance MCP server | `/pde:design-doc` â†’ `agent-skills:build` |
| EDGAR MCP server | `/pde:design-doc` â†’ `agent-skills:build` |
| `/stock-signal`, `/stock-screen`, `/stock-timing`, `/stock-model`, `/stock-equity` skills | `skill-creator:skill-creator` |
| Report UI (Vite + React) | `agent-skills:build` |

## Commands

```bash
# UI
cd ui && npm run dev        # localhost:5173
cd ui && npm run build

# MCP servers
cd mcp/yf && python server.py
cd mcp/edgar && python server.py
```

## Permissions

Per-agent local settings pre-allow `rtk ls *` and `rtk read *` â€” use these instead of bare `ls`/`cat`.

- Claude Code: `.claude/settings.local.json`
- Codex: `.codex/settings.local.json`

## Linear workflow

When working on a Linear issue:
1. **On start** â€” set the issue status to **In Progress** using `mcp__linear-server__save_issue`
2. **On complete** â€” set the issue status to **Done** using `mcp__linear-server__save_issue` only after changes have been committed and pushed

Always update Linear status. Do not leave issues in the wrong state.

## Linear wave promotion

M5 work is grouped into waves via Linear labels (`wave-1`, `wave-1.5-gate`, `wave-2`, `wave-3`, `wave-4`) and gated via `blockedBy` relationships. When a Linear issue moves to Done:

1. Run a 30-second smoke check on the just-completed work (eyeball one representative output). If it fails, the work isn't actually done â€” reopen and stop.
2. Find issues whose `blockedBy` list contained the just-completed issue â€” these are now unblocked candidates.
3. Filter unblocked candidates by wave label â€” promote in wave order; never skip a wave.
4. Promote 1â€“3 unblocked candidates to Todo, keeping the Todo queue â‰¤ 3 deep.
5. Linear's blockers enforce the hard stops (Wave 1 â†’ ABA-82 gate â†’ Wave 2 â†’ Wave 3 â†’ Wave 4); never manually bypass them.

## Git

Do not add `Co-Authored-By` trailers to commit messages.

content.text:
Audit of `AGENTS.md`:

**Stale / should remove:**

1. **Lines 55â€“63 â€” "Linear wave promotion" section.** Entirely M5-internal. M5 is done, wave-1/1.5/2/3/4 labels are historical, and we just replaced wave taxonomy with cycle + status. The ABA-82 gate reference is also stale. Whole section can go.

2. **Lines 11â€“17 â€” "Operating principle / correctness baseline / playbook layer".** These duplicate `CHARTER.md`, `COVERAGE.md`, `DESIGN.md`, and now `docs/roadmap.md`. The ABA-IDs (110/111/104/112) embedded in prose will rot. An agent only needs a one-line pointer.

**Trim:**

3. **Lines 30â€“38 â€” "Commands".** `cd mcp/yf && python server.py` is a user dev workflow, not an agent action â€” agents don't start long-running servers. UI dev commands have the same issue. Could drop the block entirely or keep just `cd ui && npm run build` (which an agent might run for verification).

**Keep as-is:**

- What this repo is (5â€“9), Build tools by component (19â€“26), Permissions (40â€“45), Linear workflow (47â€“53), Git (65â€“67) â€” all agent-facing and stable.

Want me to make the cuts? Specifically: drop wave-promotion section entirely, collapse the operating-principle block to one pointer line, and drop the Commands block (or keep only `npm run build`)?
